LAW AND MOTION 302, NO OTHER APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT APPLE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD AN ACTIONABLE MISREPRESENTATION OR A NONDISCLOSURE BASED ON AN ACTUAL DUTY TO DISCLOSE. IN AMENDING HIS COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF SHOULD KEEP IN MIND THAT DAMAGES, AS OPPOSED TO RESTITUTION, CANNOT BE RECOVERED FOR VIOLATIONS OF THE UCL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT APPLE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Continued on court's own motion to October 23, 2012. The moving party is required to provide a courtesy copy of the First Amended Complaint with a cover letter stating the new hearing date to department 302 no later than 4:00pm October 10, 2012. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-05-2012 Continued To Nov-21-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2012 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Marc R. Lewis, Esq. of Lewis & Llewelyn LLP, Tel. 415.800.0591, appearing for defendant and moving party.

No other appearance. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT APPLE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead an actionable misrepresentation or a nondisclosure based on an actual duty to disclose. In amending his complaint, plaintiff should keep in mind that damages, as opposed to restitution, cannot be recovered for violations of the UCL. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
